title: The One With The Birth Mother

lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock and Wendy Knoller
Final check by Kim
Central Perk. Everyone's sitting on the couch.Monica and Chandler enter

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're just here to say goodbye, we're off to Ohio.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, right! Your adoption interview!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yep, we're gonna meet the lady who could be carrying our baby.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't believe it. When you guys come back, you're gonna have a baby! That is so weird!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And so incorrect!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's only a couple of months pregnant. She liked our application but who knows if she's gonna like us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, she's gonna love you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhm, thank you, but we're really trying not to get our hopes up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And a lot could still get in our way.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. I mean, this girl could decide against adoption or she could like another couple better..

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you gonna name the baby?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can develop a condition in which I talk and talk and no one hears a word.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But just think, ok? What if everything goes right? What if this woman does pick you guys?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God. She's gonna pick us!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So we're standing firm on the 'not getting our hopes up'?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know, I know that things could still go wrong but if they don't? If this works out, we're gonna have a baby Chandler, a baby!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, it's gonna WORK! We're gonna make it work! I'm gonna be a mummy and (to Chandler) you're gonna be a daddy! All right, I'll see you suckers. I'm gonna get me... A BABY! {she leaves)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, screw it, I'm gonna be a daddy!!
OPENING CREDITS
Central Perk. Phoebe's speaking to a girl.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Joey) Hey, who's Phoebe with?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm gonna say someone I'm gonna have sex with. (the girl leaves and Phoebe goes toward the couch) (to Phoebe) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So... who's your friend?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, that's Sarah. No, no. Don't you get any ideas, ok? No, I'm not setting you up with any more of my friends!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: OW, why, why, why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because you'll date her once, sleep with her and then forget she exists!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, name one friend of yours that I did that with.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mandy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mandy, uh? Uh... really hot blonde, big boobs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know why I don't remember her, huh? (he winks at Rachel)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you think I'm someone else?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, I may not have treated your friends well in the past, but I have grown up a lot, really. Honest, Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, believe it or not, it's true. When Joey and I were together, he was wonderful. He was thoughtful and mature. And for the one week that we went out, he didn't sleep with anybody else!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pointing at himself) Growth!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine, I'll give you her number.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, thank you. And I promise you I will not forget this one. (he starts writing on his hand) Mandy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: SARAH!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Saraaah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey you guys, I need some fashion advice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he takes a baby blue beret out of a shopping box and puts it on) How does this look?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it's a little low... pick up a little... (Ross picks it up) a little bit more... (he picks it up again) a little bit more... (he takes it off) There you go! (pause) Now throw it away!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: C'mon! This looks good!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, please, trust me. I buy 30 fashion magazines a month. Now, I don't know who's running for president or who that... NATO guy is, but I do know that you have to get as far away as you can from that hat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damnit! I have this date tomorrow night and I have to look cool!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you know, if you want fashion help, Rachel and I are going shopping tomorrow. You're more than welcome to come with us, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really? That would be great. I mean, I have to do something, she kinda teased me about how I dress.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can see why, nice shirt!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're wearing the same shirt.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Stupid Gap on every corner!
The Adoption Agency in Ohio. Monica and Chandler are entering with a man.

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: Please, make yourself comfortable and I will back in a moment with Erica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, thank you.(the man leaves) Uh, well this is it. Are you OK?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. Just weird, you know. It's like: "Hi, I'm Chandler. May I have the human growing inside you?"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, we're gonna be great.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're gonna be great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well... obviously!

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: (he enters with Erica) Monica, Chandler. I'd like you to meet Erica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi. It is so, so nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: (whispering) Hi...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you so much for agreeing to see us.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: I'll let you get acquainted.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: So, it's Monica and Chandler. I only know you as file 0W33815-D.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what our friends call us.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Gosh, you know, you're just such an amazing couple. It's... kind of intimidating.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know about that.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: You're kidding me? I mean, it's enough that you are a doctor. But on top of it, you're married to a reverend?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (astonished) I don't think that's exactly...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (overlapping) Let her finish, doctor.
Central Perk

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, my friend Sarah had a great time last night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! So you're gonna call this one back?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you talking about? Sarah's great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, really? You know what your great friend did? We're out to dinner, ok? (he starts talking about the date and we can see what happened through a flashback video) We're getting along, having a really nice time. I was thinking she was really cool. And then, out of nowhere...
Sarah picks up some fries from Joey's plate and Joey looks very angry. Then we're back to Central Perk and Joey does a you-see-what-I-mean look to Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's it? That's why you won't go out with her again? So, she took some fries, big deal!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, look! It's not about a few fries... it's about what the fries represent.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ALL FOOD!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I can't believe I set you up with such a MONSTER!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey, hey. Look. I take a girl out, she can order whatever she wants! The more, the better! All right? Just don't order a Garden salad and then eat my food! That's a good way to lose some fingers!
Rachel enters from the main door

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Rachel) Oh

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank God you're here. Listen to this!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey and my friend were out last night and having dinner and she reaches over and takes a few of his fries...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh, no!
Joey looks satisfied

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? You know about the plate thing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah. Joey doesn't share food. I mean, just last week we were having breakfast and he had a couple of grapes on his plate and ...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Joey) You wouldn't let her have a grape?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no! Not me! Emma!
Phoebe looks horrified and she turns to watch Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (mad and pointing a finger to himself) JOEY DOESN'T SHARE FOOD!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I still think that it's a stupid reason not to call someone again. You are calling her! And if you need to, just get an extra plate of fries for the table!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (he thinks a little, considering the option and seems to be quite satisfied) I like that! A sharing buffer! Yeah! I'll order some extra fries! Maybe a plate of onion rings. Yeah. And a shrimp cocktail. And some buffalo wings. Maybe an individual pizza, uh? And some mozzarella sticks. (he looks absorbed in his food thoughts) What were we talking about?
A clothes store. Ross and Phoebe are shopping

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Ross) This place is awesome!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, we should just go, I'm not gonna find anything here! This stuff is ridiculous!
Rachel arrives with a lot of clothes

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, this place is great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, come on, I'm not gonna wear any of this! (he picks up a shirt) Nothing silver. (Rachel sighs). Ok? Nothing with hair! (Rachel sighs again) And nothing with padlocks on it! (Rachel heaves a long disappointing sigh).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, look, I know that some of this stuff is out there, but I mean, come on, look at this, look at this sweater! (she picks up a blue sweater). I mean, this is just beautiful!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (feeling the fabric) Wow, this is really soft (he looks the price). Three hundred and fifty dollars?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, down from seven hundred, you are saving like two hundred bucks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Both logic and math are taking a serious hit today.
Phoebe (walking to Ross carrying a black leather jacket): Hey, check this out! It's totally you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!
Ross wears the jackets and look at himself in the mirror

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually this looks like pretty good! Yeah!(he turns and watches his back and there's a sign on the back of the jacket, "boys will be boys") Boys will be boys?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? They will be!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, that's it, I'm getting out of here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, no, no! Ross, wait! Come on! You know, there's other stuff. Here's a nice shirt, look at these nice pants...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, actually these might look pretty good on me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, they will! You know what you should do? Just go take a walk, all right? I know your size and I'm... I'm gonna pick up some really good stuff for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! And I know what looks sexy on guys. Please, just wear what I suggest, and she's gonna go nuts for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, you're saying, uh, if I wear these pants I might be getting into hers?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Why do men keep talking to me like this?
The Adoption Agency in Ohio. Monica and Chandler are still talking with Erica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, the fact that I am a doctor, and my wife's a reverend, that's important to you?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Yeah, I read some great applications, but then I thought "who better then a minister to raise a child!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Amen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Plus I thought the baby would be in good hands with a doctor!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, good hands. (she holds Chandler hands) Healing hands.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Reverend, can I ask? Does the bible say anything about adoption?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It says "Do it!". And behold she did adopt onto them a baby. And it was good.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, wow.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I was wondering you both have such serious jobs. (to Monica) Would you have time to take care of a baby and your flock?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you know, my flock is good, I mean, yeah, my flock pretty much takes care of themselves at this point. Good flock. Flock, flock, flock.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Being a doctor must take up a lot of time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-ot for me it doesn't.
The agency guy enters the room

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: So, how's everything going in here?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: We're great, I think I may have asked all my questions.

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: Do you have any question for Erica?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, actually. So, you read a file that you liked and you gave the agency the serial number and they contacted us?

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: Yes, our system assures total anonimity. We're very proud of it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You should be. You're really on top of stuff..

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: (to Erica) Well, then if there's nothing else, then the two of us should talk.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Actually, I don't think we have to.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We don't?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Yeah, when I read about you two, I was pretty sure I wanted you, but I just thought we should meet face to face. (to the agency guy). I've made my decision. I choose them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, this is great! This is so great! (to Chandler, who looks bewildered). Did you hear that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I did.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Erica) Hey, thank you. Thank you so much. (they hugs). You are SO going to Heaven!
Joey's apartment. Rachel and Phoebe walk in, loaded with bags.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We got some really great stuff!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, yeah but I am not sure about some of the bra's I got.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Really? Do you wanna try some of them on for me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! okay. Wait, are we in Joey's imagination?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking into one of her shopping bags) Oh no! I took one of Ross' bags by
mistake, and one of mine is missing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: oh, well, Ross probably has it, you can get it from him later.
Ross walks into Central Perk, wearing a pink and white ladies shirt. Joey is on the couch

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Joey) So? What do you think? (Shows himself - Joey observes him with a strange look on his face.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think were not wearing the same shirt anymore!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (not getting it) Yeah! Yeah! Rachel picked it out for me. She told me to trust her and you know what? I'm glad I did! I turned quite a few heads on my way over here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (now laughing a little) Dude, I really don't think you should be wearing that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I see, somebody is afraid of a little competition with the ladies?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking a little agitated now) Looks like someone IS the ladies!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're just jealous because you couldn't pull this off. Yeah, now if you'll excuse me (getting up and taking his coat) I have a date. (As he is walking out, everyone turns and stares at him) See? (To Joey) ALL eyes on ME!
Monica and Chandler's hotel room. They enter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are NOT signing those papers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's wrong. They made a mistake. They think we're somebody else.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God works in mysterious ways.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You have gotta stop!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But she liked us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She likes Doctor Chandler and Reverend Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, if you think about it, I am kind of like a Reverend. I mean, as a chef, I serve God, by feeing the hungry and poor. (looks very convinced about what she just said)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Your Veal Chop is $34,95!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: C'mon Chandler, I think we have been given an opportunity. I mean, the mistake has already been made. They are writing up the paper right now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But we are not the one she chose! How can you feel okay about this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (very emotional) Because... We may not be who she thinks we are but no-one will ever love that baby more than us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know..

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, who knows how long it's gonna take for someone else to give us a baby? What if, what if no one ever picks us?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: oh, honey..

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Almost crying) Please.. please, we are so close.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica, I want a baby too, but this woman is giving away her child. She deserves to know who it's going to.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (realizes Chandler is right. She's almost crying) okay, right. (They hug)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, we'll tell the truth and who knows, maybe she'll like us for us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sniffing) Maybe she will. Uh! Why couldn't I have been a Reverend?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're Jewish.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Technicality!
A restaurant. Joey is on his date with Phoebe's friend, Sarah. They are sitting opposite each other on a table for two. Their waiter approaches with two plates.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: A garden salad for the lady (sets the plate down)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, that looks great! Good ordering!

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Seafood platter for the gentleman and extra fries. Enjoy!

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Mmmh, those fries look delicious.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: oh, I didn't know you liked French fries. Help yourself! What's mine is yours. (Sarah reaches over and takes a few fries)

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: (looks over at Joey's platter) Oh wow, are those stuffed clams?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uuuh.. yes, they are my stuffed clams.
Sarah, is grinning and starts to reach over to Joey's plate to take a few clams

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How about those fries though, huh? (Holds the plate between Sarah's fingers and his plate, thus blocking her from reaching his)

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: They are delicious (takes a few from the plate, puts one in her mouth and places the rest on her plate, then starts to reach over to Joey's platter again)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Spotting her movements takes her hand into his own) You are beautiful, you know that?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Oh, that is so sweet..

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh (grinning, trying to hold in his impatience with her) okay.. (she takes her hand back)

Then she reaches over again and Joey moves his plate a little to the left, and she misses, then she reaches out again, and he moves his plate to the right , so she misses again. She tries a third time and this time, Joey pushes his plate so far to the left, it drops off the edge of the table

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Visibly annoyed) NOW look what you did!!

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: What? what is the matter with you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't like it when people take food off of my plate, okay?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: But you just said "What's mine is yours"?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: WELL, I DIDN'T MEAN IT!

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Fine, I'm sorry, I didn't think it was that big a deal.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm overreacting. Okay, It's just when it comes to food, I have certain rules, okay, I mean (bends down and with his plate and his hands, scrapes the dropped dinner back onto the plate and puts it back on the table) There are things you do..and you now, things.. (takes something from the plate and blows it a little) that you don't do (He takes a bite from it).
Sarah looks a little disgusted
Ross and his date walk into a lobby. They are both wearing their jackets

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Wow, this place looks great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! You are gonna love it! (The girl is looking in the other direction as Ross is taking off his own coat, revealing the pink and white ladies shirt) and I'm so glad, we're finally doing this.

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Me too! (starts to take her coat off)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here (gets behind her to help. When the coat comes off we see she is wearing the exact same shirt Ross is wearing. They look at each other, shocked. They abruptly put their coat back on) So this was fun! (They leave the room and head into opposite directions)
Back at the restaurant with Joey and Sarah. Joey is holding Sarah's hands

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I really am sorry about, you know..before. I just want to make sure you know that I really do like you.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Sure (smiling) Just not as much as clams.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Jokingly) Well, stuffed clams.
The waiter arrives with their deserts

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Chocolate Torte for the lady, cheesecake for the gentleman.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, excuse me sir, there seems to be some sort of red crap on my cheesecake.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Yes, that's Raspberry coule.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (More to himself than anyone else) So stupid, ordering cheesecake, trying to
be healthy. (pushes it aside

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: (tasting hers) Oh my God! (Looks at the waiter and then to Joey)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, all right, I'll just have what she's having instead.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry sir, that was our last piece.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Mmmm! Mmm!
Sarah's beeper starts bleeping

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Oh, no! This is work. I should call in. Can you excuse me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, sure. No problem.
Joey's looking at Sarah's dessert, and takes her plate.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: What are you doing? I thought you don't share food.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure I do. (holding up his own dessert) Coule?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: (laughing) No. If I can't have your clams, you can't have my dessert. This is a two way street.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (laughing) Really?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Really! Now this all better be here when I come back. (puts her plate back at her side of the table)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, of course. I can control myself. (laughs uneasily and Sarah leaves the room)
Joey sits sideways on his chair, looking at Sarah's chocolate torte, and then looking away from it, nervously playing with his fork, drumming with it on the table every now and then.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the torte) Stop staring at me!
He then straightens himself, and looks at the torte

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why, just a tiny little...
He takes a little piece of Sarah's dessert. At first he doesn't think it's that special, but then...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh!
TIME LAPSE
Sarah enters the room again, and stops when she sees her dessert is missing. Joey has emptied her plate, and has a chocolate covered mouth, just like a kid.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm not even sorry.
The Adoption Agency in Ohio. Monica and Chandler are entering.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Adoption Agency guy
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: So, these are the preliminary forms for an open adoption. There's a lot to go over, but I'll explain everything as we go through it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pointing to a picture on the table) I-Is... Is that a picture?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Yeah. It's a sonogram they took of the baby last week. I thought you might want to see it. (gives it to Monica, who looks at it for a long time, and then shows it to Chandler)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, doctor!
Chandler takes Monica's hand, and gets serious) Look, before we sign anything we really have to talk...(pause) We're not who you think we are.

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The agency must have made some mistake. My wife is not a reverend and I'm not a doctor.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: That's impossible.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I could perform an operation on you and prove it if you'd like.

lines.speaker: Agency guy
lines.text: I'll go check your file. Excuse me.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: So who are you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, our names really are Monica and Chandler. We're from New York.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but the important thing to know about us, is how much we would care for this little baby. (holds up the sonogram)

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: So you lied to me before?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we... (makes quotation signs in the air) "bore false witness"... See I could be a reverend.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I can't believe this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But we were hoping that since we told you the truth that you still might consider...

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Giving you my baby? You think I'd give you my child after this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you don't have to decide right now, but if you could just look at our file...

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I don't want to look at your file! This is over.
She leaves the room, but Chandler runs after her. They meet in the hallway.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Erica wait!

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I've nothing to say to you. (walks a few paces)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You have every reason to be upset. We did lie. But only because we've been waiting and trying to have a baby for so long. Now we don't know how long it's gonna be before we can get another chance again.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Why don't you ask the reverend to pray on it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Erica, please. Just consider us. Ask them to see our file. Our last name's Bing. My wife's a chef and I'm in advertising.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh yeah. I actually liked you guys. But it doesn't matter, because what you did was wrong. (walks away again, but Chandler catches up with her again)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you did like us. And you should. My wife's an incredible woman. She's loving and devoted and caring. And don't tell her I said this but the woman's always right... I love my wife more than anything in this world. And I... It kills me that I can't give her a baby... I really want a kid. And when that day finally comes, I'll learn how to be a good dad. But my wife... she's already there. She's a mother... without a baby... Please?
They look at each other. We switch back to Monica. Chandler opens the door and she turns to look at him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You still want that baby?
Monica plays those words back in her mind and then smiles and runs to Chandler, who is twisting with joy. They hug.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God bless you Chandler Bing!
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Central Perk. Joey's on the couch when Ross walks to him, with his jacket closed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Turns out this sweater is made for a woman.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (nods) So, why are you still wearing it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because it's soft... Hey, so how was your date?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh... Not so good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, looks like it's just the two of us tonight, huh old buddy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and you know what? We could do a lot worse.
they shake hands the way friends would. There's a muffin on the table, and Ross breaks off a piece and wants to put it in his mouth.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shouting to Ross) JOEY DOESN'T SHARE FOOD!
Ross puts the piece back on the plate
End
